text:
Romanian Prime Minister Marcel Ciolacu has announced a boycott of the Paris 2024 Closing Ceremony after gymnast Ana Barbosu had her bronze medal stripped by an appeal from the US gymnastics team.

The Prime Minister of Romania has cancelled his attendance at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games Closing Ceremony after a Romanian athlete was stripped of her medal.

Prime Minister Marcel Ciolacu made the announcement on Tuesday after Ana Barbosu had her bronze medal position in gymnastics taken away from her.

Barbosu had finished in third place after a stunning floor exercise final but was relegated to fourth by an appeal from the United States gymnastics team.

ðŸ‡·ðŸ‡´ðŸ‡ºðŸ‡¸ Romanian gymnast Ana Barbosu realizing that the successful protest by USA's Jordan Chiles meant that she was pushed from 3rd to 4th place, not getting a medal.Ohh, noooopic.twitter.com/rrYD5dejCn

Mr Ciolacu has described the situation as “scandalous” and “dishonourable” while comparing the incident to Soviet Union corruption.

The Prime Minister said: “I decided not to participate in the closing ceremony of the Paris Olympics after the scandalous situation in gymnastics.”

“To withdraw a medal won through honest hard work on the basis of a challenge, which neither the top coaches nor the top technicians understand, is totally unacceptable!

“I remember very well how, in communism, the Russians would steal from us at the Olympics, and we’d try to argue with them with no chance of winning.”

The turmoil began when Barbosu, celebrating her bronze medal placement, was blindsided by the news her position had been overturned.

The US gymnastics team made an appeal that Jordan Chiles’ routine had been undervalued – and the jury adjusted her score from 13.666 to 13.766.

Barbosu had been posing for photos, draped in a Romanian flag, when she was seen collapsing into tears as Chiles was bumped up from fifth place to third.

The Romanian’s distress was palpable as she dropped her national flag and gripped her stomach in shock.

After the event, Prime Minister Ciolacu expressed his outrage: “I couldn't look at her tears and accept calmly that something like this is perfectly normal!”

“The fact that hundreds of millions of viewers from all over the world remained, like us Romanians, literally shocked by this terrible scene, shows… something is wrong,” he said.

“I do not want to argue with anyone today, but I only choose to protest through my gesture against a blatant injustice.”

The Prime Minister announced he would grant Barbosu the Romanian bronze medal prize bonus of €21,000 (AU$35,000) even though she officially came fourth.

Barbosu has since posted on Instagram that her Olympic experience had been “a dream come true”.

“Being in two individual finals was more than I dreamed of. Going from 8th to 4th in the floor final was exciting!” she said.

title: Romanian Prime Minister boycotts Paris 2024 Closing Ceremony over gymnastics medal controversy | Sky News Australia
description: The Prime Minister of Romania has cancelled his attendance at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games Closing Ceremony after a Romanian athlete was stripped of her medal.
keywords: ana barbosu,bercy arena,jordan chiles,romanian prime minister,paris,medal controversy,bronze medal,gymnastic,gymnastics,romania,marcel ciolacu
